results.title: GitHub - Humalike/hermes-humalike-plugin: A Hermes Agent plugin that makes your bot feel like a person in the chat instead of a bot. It plugs into the Humalike APIs so the agent knows when to speak, how to say it, who it is, and who it's talking to. · GitHub

## Repository files navigation

Humalike × Hermes

License Stars Issues Website

# Humalike Plugin

One command gives your Hermes agent social intelligence. It decides when to speak, adapts to your group's tone and remembers who said what. Works in group chats on Slack, Telegram and WhatsApp, powered by the Humalike APIs:

 Turn-taking - decides when to jump in vs stay silent, and naturalizes replies so they read like a person wrote them, not one wall of bot text.
 Persona - `/soul enhance` turns a one-line description into a fleshed-out character with a real voice.
 Theory of mind - considers how a message will land for the reader and adjusts before sending.
 Social learning - picks up the group's slang, formatting, and jokes, and starts sounding like a member rather than a visitor. [...] BranchesTags

results.content:
Report

Mateusz Jacniacki

Maker

@noctis06 Hey Noctis, these parts live on Humalike side. Both memory and classifier are on our side for few reasons:   
1. ease of integration   
2. easy way to ship improvements  
3. the Hermes plugin uses Humalike APIs that are transferable from product to product. That means if you ever wanted to create your own agent you can easily use Humalike APIs and get similar behavior as Hermes x Humalike  
  
Cost per turn is low from what we tested, when you sign up you get 10.000 free credits which should be enough for months of normal usage for hermes agent.  
  
Let me know if you are concerned about something else specifically:))

Today's models are capable enough. Smart enough. Fast enough. But we still feel they don’t fit in the room. Humalike is building the behavioral infrastructure for humanlike AI agents. The social skills & proactiveness your agents have been missing. APIs, models, benchmarks.

This is the 2nd launch from Humalike. View more

## Humalike x Hermes

Launching today

Social intelligence plugin for Hermes Agent [...] Hey PH 👋 Martí here, co-founder of Humalike.  
  
What is Humalike? The behavioral infrastructure for humanlike AI agents. The social skills your agents have been missing.  
  
Today we're shipping the fastest way to feel what that means: a plugin that makes your Hermes agent fit in 1-1's / groups.  
  
The problem  
We run Hermes agents in our own Slack and Telegram. Brilliant at tasks. Painful to use in groups and treat is as a companion. It answered every single message, talked over people, spammed 10 lines when one was enough. Everyone knew it was a bot instantly. That's not a model problem, it's a behavior problem.  
  
- What the plugin does: Decides, when to jump in and when to stay quiet  
- Paces replies like a human: typing speed, pauses, 1-3 short messages instead of a wall

results.title: Hermes plugin
results.content:
Humalike × Nous Research

HumalikeGitHub

# Make your Hermes Agent feel more Humanlike.

```
hermes update && git clone  ~/.hermes/plugins/humalike && hermes plugins enable humalike && hermes
```

View on GitHub

Engraved many-armed Hermes figure

One shot integrations:

TelegramSlackWhatsAppDiscordTelegramSlackWhatsAppDiscord

#1  Turn-taking

### Knows when to speak

Knows when to speak

Reads the chat’s rhythm and decides whether to jump in or stay silent. No wall of bot text.

#2  Persona

### Has a self

Has a self

/soul enhance turns a one-line description into a fully-voiced character, so replies sound like someone.

#3  Theory of mind

### Reads the room

Reads the room

Weighs how a message will land for the person on the other end, and adjusts before sending.

results.title: MY DEEP TECHNICAL EXPLORATION AND PERSONAL EXPERIENCE WITH HERMES AGENT - DEV Community
results.content: The world of artificial intelligence is moving incredibly fast. Most tools available today are simple chatbot wrappers. You talk to them, they forget everything you said, and you have to start over the next time you log in. I always found this frustrating. I wanted a persistent digital co-worker. I wanted something that remembers my projects, learns from its mistakes, and runs on my own infrastructure instead of someone else's cloud. This is exactly what Hermes Agent delivers. It is an open-source, self-improving artificial intelligence agent built by the talented team at Nous Research.

results.title: A little sad about Hermes
results.content:
# A little sad about Hermes

## Post details
r/hermesagent | 249 upvotes | 213 comments | Posted by u/SavaStone on 2026-06-13T21:57:09.979Z

## Content
I feel sad that I can't find a proper use for the agent in my life. Answers from the internet? Regular AI chats will do the trick. Code? Coding agents work better and faster. Working with my calendar and tasks? It's faster for me to do it myself. I set up various things and profiles in it, but the agent does many things worse than I do, and it didn’t help my productivity in any way. But I'm interested in the technology itself. How exactly has it helped you? What REALLY useful use cases do you know and use?
